results.title: 8 Best Inboxally Alternatives — 2026 - Mailmend: The Best Email Deliverability Software
results.content:
Warmy.io positions itself as a comprehensive warmup solution with advanced AI capabilities and one of the largest seed inbox networks in the market.

Key Features:

35,000+ real inbox network for engagement simulation

GPT-4 powered content generation creating natural warmup conversations

Automated DNS configuration for SPF, DKIM, and DMARC setup

Provider-specific optimization for Gmail, Outlook, and Yahoo

Real-time blacklist monitoring across 50+ major lists

Deliverability forecasting with predictive analytics

Pricing Structure:

Volume-based pricing

Available in options: B2B Sender, B2C Sender, & Custom Plan

The platform's extensive inbox network and AI capabilities make it suitable for businesses requiring comprehensive warmup across multiple email providers. [...] Implementation Process:

The platform operates through three-step technical execution:

Sending client emails to rotating test inboxes to determine current placement

Using proprietary AI to analyze promotional threshold factors

Generating custom code that embeds into Klaviyo templates

For e-commerce brands frustrated by promotional tab placement reducing email visibility and revenue, Mailmend eliminates the content modification requirements and multi-week timelines associated with traditional deliverability solutions. The platform serves 500+ e-commerce businesses across personal care, apparel, home goods, food & beverage, and lifestyle accessories.

## 2. Warmy.io — AI-Powered Warmup with 35,000+ Inbox Network [...] Agency packages with volume discounts

Mailreach excels at providing visibility into deliverability metrics that other warmup tools treat as black boxes. Users can see exactly how emails perform across different providers, identifying whether Gmail placement succeeds while Outlook flags messages as spam. However, some users report interface performance issues during high-volume sending periods.

## 8. Mails.ai — Enterprise Warmup with Vast Business Customer Base

Mails.ai serves as an all-in-one cold email platform with robust warmup capabilities, supporting businesses by sending billions of emails monthly through advanced warmup plans.

Platform Capabilities:

Unlimited email account connections

Advanced AI-based spam detection and optimization

### Zapmail Alternative: All Three Infrastructure Types

Zapmail charges $2.99-3.50/inbox for Google and Microsoft. MailDeck adds SMTP and starts at $0.30/inbox. Full pricing comparison at every scale.

### ScaledMail Alternative: Deeper Scale Across Providers

ScaledMail Outlook costs $2.00/inbox with 25 per domain. MailDeck runs 100 per tenant at $0.30/inbox. Full pricing at every scale inside.

results.title: MTG Agents — AI Deck Builder & Rules Assistant for MTG
results.content:
### Is MTG Agents free to use?

Yes! MTG Agents is completely free. You can chat with our AI assistants for rules questions and deck building advice without any subscription or payment. Create an account to save your decks and chat history.

### How is MTG Agents different from using ChatGPT for Magic questions?

General AI chatbots like ChatGPT often hallucinate card names and get rules wrong. MTG Agents is purpose-built for Magic: The Gathering with specialized agents, real-time card data from Scryfall, and direct integration with official rules sources. Every answer comes with verifiable citations, and every card suggestion is a real, playable card.

### What MTG formats do the AI agents support? [...] ## Frequently Asked Questions

Common questions about MTG Agents and how our AI assistants can help you. For more detailed rules questions, visit our comprehensive MTG Rules FAQ or explore our Commander Deck Building Guide.

### What is MTG Agents?

MTG Agents is a free AI chatbot built specifically for Magic: The Gathering players. Unlike general-purpose AI, it features specialized MTG AI agents — Nissa for rules questions and Karn for deck building — that provide instant, accurate answers with cited sources from official MTG resources.

### Is there an AI bot for Magic: The Gathering? [...] ### Is there an AI bot for Magic: The Gathering?

Yes — MTG Agents is a purpose-built AI bot for MTG. It's not a generic chatbot repurposed for Magic; it's designed from the ground up with access to Scryfall's 40,000+ card database, the Comprehensive Rules, and community sources like RulesGuru. You can ask rules questions, build decks, and get card suggestions — all for free.

### Can Karn help me build a Commander deck?

Yes! Karn is MTG Agents' AI deck building specialist. You can upload your Commander deck, and he'll analyze it for synergies, suggest improvements based on real player data, and recommend cards that fit your strategy and budget. He can also build a complete deck from scratch — just describe what you want.

### Is MTG Agents free to use?

results.title: Deck (building) - Wikipedia
results.content:
In architecture, a deck is a flat surface capable of supporting weight, similar to a floor, but typically constructed outdoors, often elevated from the ground, and usually connected to a building. The term is a generalization from the deck "Deck (ship)") of a ship. A level architectural deck may be intended for use by people, e.g., what in the UK is usually called a decked patio. "Roof deck" refers to the flat layer of construction materials to which the weather impervious layers are attached to form a roof, and they may be either level (for a "flat" rooftop) or sloped.

## Functions and materials

[edit&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Functions and materials")] [...] [edit&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Construction")]

The deck of a house is generally a wooden platform built above the ground and connected to the main building. It is generally enclosed by a railing for safety. Access may be from the house through doors and from the ground via a stairway. Residential decks can be constructed over steep areas or rough ground that is otherwise unusable. Decks can also be covered by a canopy "Canopy (building)") or pergola to control sunlight. Deck designs can be found in numerous books, do-it-yourself magazines, and websites, and from the USDA. [...] Wood or timber decking can be used in a number of ways: as part of garden landscaping, to extend the living area of a house, and as an alternative to stone-based features such as patios. Decks are made from treated lumber, composite lumber, composite material, and aluminum. Lumber may be western red cedar, teak, mahogany, ipê, reclaimed and recycled ulin and other hardwoods. Recycled planks may be high-density polyethylene (HDPE), polystyrene (PS) and PET plastic as well as mixed plastics and wood fiber (often called "composite" lumber). Artificial decking products are often called "wood-plastic composites" (WPC). WPCs have different brands like NewTechWood, Azek, Ecornboard etc.
